In the following question, a part of the sentence is given in bold. Choose the correct alternative that best improves the bold part. If no improvement is needed, select “No improvement.”
The Vice Chancellor congratulated the blind student for his success in the examination.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
The verb “congratulate” is typically followed by the preposition “on” when referring to the reason for the congratulation.
Correct sentence: “The Vice Chancellor congratulated the blind student on his success in the examination.”

Q9 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Read the passage carefully and choose the best answer to the following question from the four alternatives given below.
In the past 50 years, doctors across the world have accepted the practice to prescribe antibiotics at the first sign of a trivial infection or treat patients with a handful of antibiotics. These days it is not uncommon to see practitioners prescribing multiple antibiotics without any real indication or relevance for such a combination of drugs. Antibiotics have traditionally been known as miracle drugs, but there is growing evidence that they are overworked miracles, especially in countries like ours where there is easy access to drugs across the counter, including antibiotics. We cannot think of a return to pre-antibiotic days. Yet the unbridled use of these agents is inexorably propelling us in that direction.
Antibiotics are called overworked miracles because:

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The passage clearly points out that antibiotics are being excessively used, so they are called overworked miracles.

Q27 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Identify the correct statement when LR parsers compared using any particular grammar.

Correct answer: D, A
Solution

(d)
If grammar is LR (0) then it is also SLR (1). It implies if no conflict present in LR (0) then SLR (1)
also don’t have any conflict.
If CLR (1) has no conflict then LALR (1) may have SR conflict.
Hence option (d) is correct.

The value returned by the function “find” when a pointer to the root of a non-empty tree is passed as argument is
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The given program recursively find the number of levels in left sub tree and number of levels in
right sub tree of root node, then at last it will add 1 + max (levels in left sub tree, levels in right sub tree).
Which will result number of levels in given tree.

Q46 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A multi-threaded application contains three threads T1, T2 and T3 within a single process
Consider the following events:
I. T1 performs a blocking I/O operation.
II. The operating system provides no kernel-level thread support (threads are entirely at user level).
III. A thread scheduler is implemented within the process.
Which of the following statements is correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• With user-level threads, the OS is unaware of the existence of threads — it only sees the process.
• If one thread makes a blocking system call, the entire process blocks, causing all threads in
that process to stop until the blocking operation finishes.

Q56 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following is false?

Correct answer: D, A, B, C
Solution

(d)
(a) Non-deterministic TM and deterministic TM are equivalent.
(b) Complement of a CFL is recursive and recursive language is recognized by Turing Machine.
So, complement of a context free language can be recognized by a TM.
(c) If a language is recursively enumerable, there exists a TM which outputs “yes” whenever a
string is in the language. If its complement is recursively enumerable, there exists a TM which
outputs “yes” whenever the string is not in the language. So, we can have a “yes” if a string
belongs to the language and “no” if a string does not belong to the language ⇒ language is recursive.
(d) If a language is non-recursively enumerable, its complement may be recursively enumerable
(can be recognized by a Turing Machine).

Q59 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
• In write-through + no-write-allocate, a write miss writes directly to main memory without
caching the block. So, option (a) is correct.
• In write-back, dirty blocks are written to main memory, not to secondary storage, when
replaced. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• Write-allocate fetches the block into the cache on a write miss before updating it. So, option
(c) is correct.
• Write-back + write-allocate loads the block on a write miss and delays main memory update
until block replacement. So, option (d) is correct.
